title: excoecaria lissophylla
intro: Excoecaria lissophylla, also known as the poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities. The leaves and sap of the Excoecaria lissophylla plant have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, providing benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Excoecaria lissophylla often involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and sap, which can be used topically or ingested under the guidance of a qualified practitioner.
